paper_title: Benzimidazole synthesis via oxidative condensation of 1,2-diaminoarenes with primary amines using MOF-235 as an effective heterogeneous catalyst

entities.description: The primary field of research concerning the synthesis of chemical compounds and catalysts.
The process of creating benzimidazole derivatives, which are important in agrochemicals and pharmaceuticals.
A type of catalysis where the phase of the catalyst differs from that of the reactants.
A chemical reaction involving the combination of two molecules with the loss of small molecules, accompanied by oxidation.
An iron-based metal-organic framework used as a heterogeneous catalyst.
A method used to synthesize MOF-235 from 1,4-benzenedicarboxylic acid and iron(III) chloride.
Gas chromatography-mass spectrometry used for product identification.
Equipment used for recording 1H NMR and 13C NMR spectra.
The solvent found to be most effective for the reaction.
A digital database used to compare and identify MS spectra.
Yield of the synthesized MOF-235 catalyst.
Highest reaction yield for 2-phenyl-1H-benzo[d]imidazole using MOF-235 in chlorobenzene.
Reaction yield monitored without the presence of a catalyst.
Researcher at Ho Chi Minh City University of Technology.
Researcher at Ho Chi Minh City University of Technology.
Researcher at Ho Chi Minh City University of Technology.
Researcher affiliated with both Ho Chi Minh City University of Technology and Can Tho University.
Corresponding author from Ho Chi Minh City University of Technology.
